reachability_artifacts: built sworn capabilities plus real provider request capture before login, during login, and after logout, proving identical registry identity, endpoint, and customer credential source
built direct OpenRouter and subscription-driver gestures proving both retained paths without account state

effort_complexity.rationale: See spec.json; the implementer must confirm every dispatch and enumeration consumer of the retired proxy predicate.

release_benefit: A SwornAgent user can use account identity without losing provider credentials, changing model routes, purchasing managed inference, or generating unapproved outbound traffic.

positive_scenarios: A direct provider route and a subscription route remain identical across login and logout.
A customer-owned OpenRouter key remains a normal direct provider credential.
negative_scenarios: Account presence cannot change a registry choice, endpoint, or API key.
Retired proxy environment variables cannot retain hidden runtime authority.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: Making explicit prefixes the only route authority removes hidden spend and trust changes from account gestures.

design_decisions.choice: Remove managed proxy routing rather than preserve a dormant partner seam.
options: remove routing and require future ratification
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified 2026-07-15: remove Sworn-managed proxy routing; execution remains direct/BYO or explicit subscription driver.
design_decisions.rationale: Dormant bearer, metering, and route-switching code remains a trust liability without an approved service boundary.

design_decisions.choice: Remove SWORN_DIRECT immediately with no recognized no-op.
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified 2026-07-15: remove all active recognition; direct routing becomes invariant and no replacement flag is introduced.
design_decisions.rationale: Existing users remain direct, while dead configuration would imply another route still exists.
